notes: Strongest skid/flip ball in Brunswick lineup; versatile not strongest overall
Reads earlier/more aggressive than base Combat; reaction shape strength 94/100
Quicker start and more total hook than Ivory Rhino Pro
Reaction shape strength 81/100
Reaction shape strength 93/100; one of strongest Brunswick balls ever; DynamiCore2
Bridges gap between responsive pearls and slower solids
Reaction shape strength 82/100; cleaner and more continuous than solid version
Modern remake of 1996 original; house-shot oriented
Cleaner and sharper than solid Danger Zone
Reaction shape strength 62/100; more angular than Teal Rhino Pro
Reaction shape strength 90/100; hybrid version of Vaporize core paired with Hypnotize-style cover
Reaction shape strength 91/100; bridges gap between Mesmerize and Vaporize
Dullest/earliest of the three Ethos versions
Earlier and more continuous than base Ethos pearl; good benchmark ball
USBC-legal harder urethane; more flare than standard urethane balls
Positioned between Severe Collision and Heckler Hybrid; smoother/rounder than Severe. Exact RG/diff not separately published - assumed same as Collision core family; releases Aug 2026
Pearl complement to base Hater; clears fronts easier while retaining strength
Possibly most angular Collision release; first to use Cohesion+Havoc Hybrid blend
No-thumb bowler focused design; first DV8 ball with Havoc cover absent HK22 base
Higher RG core keeps rough box finish from burning up too early
Pearlized follow-up to Trouble Maker; more length and sharper reaction to friction
First DV8 ball with Cohesion additive; one of strongest hooking shiny(solid) balls in lineup
More length and more angular backend than Verge Solid
Cleaner and more angular version of Brutal Collision; strong asym pearl
Introduced HK22 base cover to Collision core; devastating backend reaction
Original Collision line ball; note: one retailer listing mislabels core as "Symmetric" - reviews consistently describe it as asymmetric
Most aggressive symmetric DV8 ball at time of release; same core/cover family as base 2019 Verge Solid - possibly a colorway/relaunch
Lower differential evolution of Verge Solid; very versatile across angles
Cleaner pearl version of Damn Good Verge; same core
Higher RG than original Turmoil core; strong yet controllable
Rougher box finish than original Turmoil Solid; more back end continuation
Third ball in Turmoil series; smoother than Turmoil Pearl despite hybrid cover
DV8's strongest symmetric ball at release. Very early midlane read with a smooth, continuous motion. Excellent on heavy oil and fresh sport patterns; controllable rather than angular.
Pearlized cover gives significantly more length than Turmoil Solid
Modified Freakshow-style core; picks up lane earlier than Turmoil Pearl
Cleaner look through the front than the GB5 Solid while keeping very similar total hook; natural follow-up to the GB5 Pearl
Heavy-oil benchmark of the Pro Performance line; strong early read with continuous, more angular finish than a typical solid
Same Entity core as the solid version but cleaner cover clears the front better for a stronger, longer move off friction
More length and a sharper change of direction than the GB4 Hybrid; targeted at medium oil
Reminiscent of the GB4 but with more continuation thanks to a stronger cover intended for more traction and hook in oil

finish: 500/1000/1500 Siaair Micro Pad / Crown Factory Compound
notes: First Game Breaker to use the HK22 coverstock base; sits between the GB4 Solid and GB4 Pearl in length and motion
Pearl companion to the GB4 Solid; floats longer down the lane while the Enhanced V2 core retains energy for a strong backend
Re-release of the classic 2014 Game Breaker 2 benchmark; smoother and cleaner than the GB4 Solid
Recreated the classic Enhanced V2 core shape/numbers behind the Game Breaker line's success; wrapped in DynamiCore
Colorway release of the base GB4 Solid; same Enhanced V2 core and GB 12.0 Solid cover specs
First change to the V2 core shape since the Game Breaker 2; shaved sides create an intermediate differential for the most angular Game Breaker release to date
Built to be longer and more angular than the GB3 Pearl using a polished hybrid version of the GB cover
Sanded hybrid cover from the Matrix reused on the GB3 lineup; predictable continuous motion on medium-heavy oil
Red-pin colorway/production variant of the GB3 Hybrid; same Enhanced V2 core and cover specs
Sits between Destiny Solid and Destiny Pearl in length and backend motion; third coverstock option in the Lower Mid Performance Destiny line
Skids through the front and makes a bigger move off the dry at the back compared to Destiny Solid
Entry-level Destiny release; dull sanded finish reads earliest of the three Destiny coverstocks
Replacement for the discontinued Phenom; sanded hybrid cover produces more total hook than the Game Breaker 2
Most predictable pearl in the line at the time; more length through the front while keeping strong mid-lane/backend continuation of the GB3 Solid
Strongest Black Widow to date; more length and sharper flip than base 3.0

Same core as the Purple Pearl Urethane, but reactive shell raises differential vs. the urethane version, giving more flare/stronger backend than the urethane while staying a "benchmark"-style controllable ball. High RG (2.586) keeps overall hook potential modest despite the reactive cover.
Created much more length than original solid Hammerhead; covers fewer boards
"Ghost" appears to be a colorway name for the standard Zero Mercy Pearl; core specs match base Pearl release
Colorway of base Zero Mercy Solid; dull finish reads very early
Colorway of base Zero Mercy Solid - same core/cover

core_name: Gas Mask Low Diff (flip block removed)
notes: First-ever Tour edition Black Widow; much lower diff than standard Widows
Third release in 3-D Offset line
Urethane-style control ball; not actual urethane so unrestricted by 78D hardness rule
Cleaner and quicker response than Zero Mercy Solid
Strongest heavy-oil ball in current Hammer lineup per reviewers
Lower diff than base Effect for more control on medium oil
Pearl variant of Extreme Envy (base is CFI Solid)
Beloved benchmark/tournament ball; lower diff than original Envy
Released April 28 2026; EJ Tackett tribute rebuilding the Covert Revolt's Vanquish core with a modernized solid cover for medium-heavy oil; near-max USBC differential drives heavy track flare
Released April 21 2026; upgrade of the Top Thrill line; Halogen core returns after 6 years with a 42% higher differential than the Top Thrill's Halogen V2
Released January 20 2026; solid companion to the Evoke Hysteria; first-ever Propulsion MXV Solid, reengineered from the pearl version with Duramax additive
Released March 31 2026; pairs the proven benchmark Gear core with the exact Propulsion MXV Pearl cover/colors of the 2025 Ball of the Year Evoke Hysteria
Released December 16 2025; introduces the dual-density Apex Predator core with patent-pending Power Band technology; highest RG of any Predator core to date
Released January 21 2025; first Venom to use Duramax additive technology; benchmark light-medium oil control piece
Released July 22 2025; wraps the Detonator core in the Evoke Hysteria's Propulsion MXV Pearl cover for medium-heavy oil
Released February 25 2025; Motiv's 2025 Ball of the Year; tunable Overload core lets drillers raise diff ~20% or lower it for more control
Released August 20 2024; strongest coverstock on a Forge to date with Duramax additive; reads earlier than Forge Ember
Released January 23 2024; original Evoke; introduced the tunable Overload core, adjustable up to 25% differential via drilling
Released December 17 2024; Leverage HXC cover evolved from the Subzero Forge's Leverage MXC with Duramax additive; Length 53 / Backend 70 / Hook 87
Released February 28 2023; modern update to the Venom Shock; benchmark light-medium oil piece for rev-dominant bowlers
Released December 12 2023; cleanest asymmetric pearl Jackal at the time; fast response even in heavy oil
Released October 19 2021; strongest pearl symmetric Motiv had made to date; shares the angular Infusion MVP cover with the Ripcord Velocity
Releases August 13 2026; keeps the Evil Eye's tunable Super Symmetrical core but wraps it in a new pearl cover for more down-lane length and a sharper backend flip
Releases June 30 2026; benchmark control piece delivering a urethane-style smooth roll while still absorbing oil like reactive resin; legal for all USBC/PBA play
Released October 14 2025; Radical's bestseller; distinctive core protrusions give it the widest intermediate-differential drilling range (.009-.023) ever seen on a symmetrical ball
Released October 14 2025; modern reissue of the classic best-selling Intel; measured about 3 boards stronger than the original thanks to the updated HK22 cover
Released December 30 2025; adds the Cohesion-additive HK22C cover to the proven Outer Limits core for more front-of-lane length and a sharper, more defined breakpoint
Released January 5 2025; solid-cover version that increased the overall hook potential of the Outer Limits line while keeping its long-axis-rotation-retaining shape
Released February 21 2023; original release of the line; core numbers developed with a new method Radical used to predict ball motion
Released May 21 2019; EZ-Use line; provided the most length in Radical's lineup at the time; spiritual successor to the Rack Attack Pearl
Released May 21 2019; EZ-Use line; higher-RG/lower-diff symmetric core gives tight flare rings and easy length on drier conditions; successor to the Rack Attack Solid
Benchmark control piece; re-engineered from original Gremlin for lower flare
Predecessor to Gremlin Tour-X; highest RG/diff ever for the Rondure core family; sits just below Attention Star, above Gremlin Tour-X in overall strength
Nano-free pearl; cleaner and longer than base solid RST Hyperdrive
Benchmark symmetric; fills gap between MicroTrax and eTrax coverstocks
Pearl version of Rockstar; clears fronts easier with sharper backend
First HP5 line ball; core dynamics change significantly based on drilling (wing removed or not)
Re-release of original Idol with AI core added; very similar performance to original
First domestic Idol-series ball with MicroTrax Pearl cover; ~2-3 ft more length than solid
Revives classic Cell/Nucleus core; strong heavy-oil performer
Momentous core previously overseas-only; brought domestic with AI core added
Entry-level HP1 line; higher RG gives more length before hook phase
Lowest RG symmetric core in Roto Grip history; cleaner and more angular than original solid TNT
Chemically charged nano cover gives more responsiveness than original Idol; ~2-3 boards more hook
This is the original/base US-market Idol (Magenta is its standard color); domestic release confirmed
Domestic release (2018); complements base Idol with more length and stronger backend pop
SPI Lab series urethane alternative; released Feb 27 2026; very low flare high RG core with aggressive oil-absorbing pearl cover
2026 colorway release of the Ion Max; same core/cover specs as original Sept 2024 Neon Pink/Indigo/White version
Debuted Storm's Adhesion System (A1S) coverstock; built for 39-44ft league/tournament patterns; classic skid-flip shape
Same Solarion core as pearl Equinox but earlier-reading A2S solid cover; hook comparable to Ion Max with different shape
40th-anniversary Hy-Road release pairing classic Inverted Fe2 core with newer A1S pearl cover; more predictable than original Hy-Road Pearl
Classic Black/Ultramarine Blue Hy-Road colorway; benchmark hybrid symmetric for medium-heavy oil
Limited public specs available for this colorway/variant; assumed to share the standard Hy-Road Inverted Fe2 core and cover family
Pearl version of Ion Max; same Element Max A.I. core but cleaner cover for more length and sharper finish
Solid-cover version of Ion Pro; reads earlier and hooks more than the hybrid Ion Pro
Red-White-Blue colorway of Ion Pro Solid; identical core/cover specs to standard Ion Pro Solid
Pearl version of the legendary Phaze II; same Velocity core but stronger backend motion and more length than the solid original
Modern A.I.-core update to the benchmark !Q Tour; hybrid cover grabs more in the mids than expected for the nameplate
Follow-up release sharing the Supercoil core and EXO Pearl cover family with the original DNA Coil
NeX cover is Storm's earliest-reading solid to date
Original Sept 2024 release; Neon Pink/Indigo/White; over 50% more flare potential than Ion Pro
Benchmark asymmetric introducing the Element Tour A.I. core; hybrid cover version predates Ion Pro Solid
Third and most forgiving Summit release; R2S solid cover is cleaner through the front than original Summit's hybrid
Solid-cover version of the Absolute; more midlane read than original while keeping the confident backend
Storm's strongest symmetric ball at release; low RG plus high diff Centripetal HD core with Amplified Inertia outer core
Pearl version of the Summit; same core
Pearl remake of the classic !Q Tour lineage (following Gold/30/Emerald); cleanest and flippiest of the IQ pearls
Longtime benchmark/PBA staple; low-RG high-diff Velocity core with solid TX-16 cover for medium-heavy oil
